skill.name: resmi-yazi
skill.description: Creates Turkish official letters, petitions, objection letters, notices, and institutional correspondence using formal local conventions.
skill.summary: Drafts Turkish official petitions and business correspondence from user details.
skill.author: cesareth

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick fences as shell execution and prose references as weak cryptography. Review found no executable code, network behavior, filesystem access, credential handling, or prompt injection in SKILL.md. The skill is a text-generation guide for Turkish official correspondence, with privacy and legal-consultation cautions already present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings at SKILL.md lines 37, 65, 68, 94, 97, 131, 134, 158, 161, 182, and 186 are Markdown code fences around document templates. They do not execute commands and do not process user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are triple-backtick Markdown fences in a prose-only SKILL.md file. No Ruby, shell, subprocess, or executable script context is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3, 96, 117, 130, and 150 occur in Turkish prose and template text. No hashing, encryption, crypto library, or security-sensitive algorithm is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown guidance and sample official-letter text. There is no cryptographic operation or code path where a weak algorithm could be invoked.

content.value_statement: Writing formal Turkish petitions and institutional letters can be difficult without the right structure. This skill provides clear templates and wording for official correspondence.
actual_capabilities: Drafts general Turkish petitions with institution, subject, request, signature, and attachment sections.
Creates SGK service document request letters from user-provided employment details.
Prepares tax objection, resignation, tenant notice, and job application letter templates.
Guides users through required information before drafting a formal letter.
Uses formal Turkish wording for government, company, and notary-related correspondence.
Warns users about identity-number privacy and the need for legal advice in legal matters.
limitations: It does not submit documents to any public institution or company.
It does not verify current laws, deadlines, or official form requirements.
It is not a substitute for a lawyer, accountant, or licensed professional.
Users must review all personal data before sharing or filing a document.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Government Petition
use_cases.description: Create a structured Turkish petition for a public office, including the request, reason, signature block, and attachments.

use_cases.title: Draft an Administrative Objection
use_cases.description: Prepare a formal objection letter for tax or administrative decisions using the user’s dates, reference numbers, and reasons.

use_cases.title: Standardize Workplace Letters
use_cases.description: Draft resignation notices, job application letters, and company correspondence in a consistent formal style.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Petition Draft
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple petition
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Turkish petition for [institution]. My request is [request]. My reason is [reason]. Include placeholders for personal details and attachments.

prompt_templates.title: SGK Service Document Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a formal SGK service document request. Use these details: workplace [name], employment dates [dates], city [city], and requested document [document].

prompt_templates.title: Tax Decision Objection
prompt_templates.scenario: Objecting to an administrative tax decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Turkish objection letter to [tax office]. Decision date: [date]. Decision number: [number]. Objection reasons: [reasons]. Requested outcome: [outcome].

prompt_templates.title: Full Formal Correspondence Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review and rewrite this Turkish official letter for formal tone, clear request, complete sections, privacy risks, and missing attachments: [letter text].

output_examples.input: I need a petition to request a document from a municipality.
output_examples.output: A formal Turkish petition with city and date, municipality heading, subject line, request paragraph, reason paragraph, signature block, and attachment list.

output_examples.input: I want to resign from my job and give notice.
output_examples.output: A resignation notice addressed to human resources, with start date, role, intended leaving date, notice-period wording, and contact details.

output_examples.input: Check my official letter before I submit it.
output_examples.output: A revised version with formal wording, clearer request language, missing information notes, and privacy warnings for identity numbers.

best_practices: Provide the institution name, request, reason, dates, and attachment list before drafting.
Use placeholders for national identity numbers until the final private copy.
Confirm legal deadlines and document requirements with the relevant institution or a professional.
anti_patterns: Do not include unnecessary identity numbers or sensitive personal data in early drafts.
Do not treat generated legal wording as professional legal advice.
Do not submit a letter without checking names, dates, addresses, and reference numbers.

faq.question: Can this skill write Turkish petitions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft formal Turkish petitions with headings, request text, reasons, signature details, and attachments.

faq.question: Does it submit documents for me?
faq.answer: No. It only drafts and reviews text. You must submit documents through the correct official channel.

faq.question: Can it provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It can help with wording, but legal matters should be reviewed by a qualified professional.

faq.question: Can I use it for SGK and tax letters?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for SGK service document requests and tax objection letters.

faq.question: Should I include my Turkish identity number?
faq.answer: Use a placeholder while drafting. Add sensitive personal data only when necessary and only in your final private document.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the recipient institution, request, reason, key dates, personal role, contact details, and attachment list.
